name: CHOLEDOCHAL CYST
definition: CYSTIC DILATATION OF EXTRAHEPATIC BILE DUCT rare congenital biliary tract anomaly characterized by dilatation of all / part of the extra- / multiple saccular dilatations of EBD only

demographics.Prevalence: 1÷13,000 admissions; high prevalence in Japanese / Asian infants
demographics.Age: < 10 years (60%) + young adulthood; 80% diagnosed in childhood; 7% during pregnancy;
demographics.Histo: fibrous cyst wall without epithelial lining

distribution: NO / mild peripheral intrahepatic bile duct dilatation
discriminating_features: NO / mild peripheral intrahepatic bile duct dilatation
anterior displacement of 2nd portion of duodenum + distal portion of stomach (on LAT
widening of C-loop with inferior displacement of duodenum (on AP view)

general: NO / mild peripheral intrahepatic bile duct dilatation
anterior displacement of 2nd portion of duodenum + distal portion of stomach (on LAT
widening of C-loop with inferior displacement of duodenum (on AP view)
US: ballooned / fusiform cyst beneath porta hepatis separate from gallbladder
abrupt change of caliber at junction of dilated segment to normal duct
right-sided cyst in fetal abdomen + adjacent dilated hepatic ducts
photopenic area within liver that fills within 60 min + stasis of tracer within cyst
lack of tracer passage into small intestine
prominent hepatic ductal activity ← dilatation of ducts
anomalous pancreaticobiliary junction with long common channel
